channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Karl Urban Wants To Play John Marston In A Red Dead Redemption Film
item.content:encoded:
It’s a testament if your career kicks off with Lord of the Rings and only gets dorkier from there. New Zealand actor Karl Urban has stepped into starring gigs in Star Trek, Judge Dredd, The Boys and in short time Johnny Cage in Mortal Kombat. But there’s one more feather Urban would like in his cap. It happens to be a video game character whose cap already has a feather in it: John Marston.

In an interview with Square Mile magazine, the actor said if he had his choice of any video game adaptation, it would be as John Marston in Red Dead Redemption. “I’d love to play that character,” said Urban. “Red Dead is a fantastic game. It’s interesting because I didn’t really have too much of an opportunity to get that heavily invested in the gaming culture… when I did get the rare opportunity, Red Dead was certainly one of the games that spoke to me.”

It goes without saying that Urban wanting to star in a Red Dead Redemption film does not mean an adaptation of the 2010 classic is in the pipeline. In fact, Rockstar seems to be one of the few major developers not chasing cross-media expansion (though they may have their hands full with Grand Theft Auto VI). Still, stranger things have happened than making a little wish. Kirsten Dunst was recently added to the cast of Minecraft 2 after telling Town & Country it was her dream role a year prior. Albeit because she wanted to “make a movie where I don’t lose money.”

The final season of The Boys began earlier this week. Urban will also be putting on Johnny Cage’s shades in Mortal Kombat II when it comes out in May. He told Square Mile to expect a slightly more humble rendition of the character, if only because Cage is left baggy a deflated ego. “He is a man that the world has forgotten about,” said Urban. “To me, that was really fertile ground to launch the character into. That gives me somewhere to go as an actor.”



https://kotaku.com/karl-urban-wants-to-play-john-marston-in-a-red-dead-redemption-film-2000686877
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Dragon Age: Inquisition Servers Going Offline Later This Month
item.content:encoded:
In an era where online multiplayer games can barely go a few weeks before shutting down, it’s staggering to think of a largely single player experience sundowning its servers after 12 years. But such is the case for Dragon Age: Inquisition. Originally released in 2014, EA has announced the PS3 servers for the game will be shutting down on April 28th, ending its multiplayer features.

Inquisition was the third game in the mainline Dragon Age series but the first to introduce multiplayer features, opening up the solo series to co-operative campaigns with friends. Inquisition was critically acclaimed and sold extremely well, remaining one of Bioware’s strongest works. The same cannot be said of Bioware’s live-service exploits since then.

Anthem, Bioware’s more ambitious open world multiplayer game, failed to draw players away from Destiny or No Man’s Sky. A fumble, the 2019 game ended support only two years later, with its servers shutting down earlier this year. The latest Dragon Age, Veilguard, would have likely been a live-service experience as well had Anthem performed better. While kindly received by critics and selling well at launch, The Veilguard’s long-term prospects failed to meet EA’s expectations. Layoffs at Bioware followed shortly after.

Now that gaming is defined by not which games are good but which games survive, it’s nostalgic to see off Inquisition on more graceful terms. Highguard couldn’t cut it for more than two months and Concord was snuffed out after just two weeks, the biggest bomb in PlayStation history. Meanwhile, Dragon Age: Inquisition left their door open for over a decade and the PSN has been ringing out orders for 20 years. The only tragedy is how it illustrates how shortsighted on current industry can be, chasing short term thrills at the cost of longterm potential. EA hasn’t clarified if the decision is the result of their layoffs or effects it’ll have on the Dragon Age Keep community portal.



https://kotaku.com/dragon-age-inquisition-servers-going-offline-later-this-month-2000686871
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
